questions.q: Who founded and edited the nationalist newspaper Kesari?
questions.explanation: Bal Gangadhar Tilak founded and edited Kesari, using it to spread assertive nationalist ideas.

questions.q: In which language was Tilak's newspaper Kesari published?
questions.explanation: Kesari was published in Marathi, while Tilak's companion paper Maratha was in English.

questions.q: What does the word 'Kesari', the name of Tilak's newspaper, mean?
questions.explanation: 'Kesari' means lion, signalling the paper's bold and aggressive nationalist tone.

questions.q: Tilak edited Kesari along with which English-language newspaper?
questions.explanation: Tilak ran the English-language Maratha alongside the Marathi Kesari.

questions.q: Which newspaper did Mahatma Gandhi use to spread his ideas in English?
questions.explanation: Gandhi edited Young India to communicate his nationalist and social ideas in English.

questions.q: Which journal did Gandhi launch focused on the upliftment of the depressed classes?
questions.explanation: Gandhi launched Harijan to advocate for the upliftment of those he called Harijans.

questions.q: The nationalist press of the freedom era is best described as a tool for what?
questions.explanation: Newspapers like Kesari became powerful instruments of political awakening against colonial rule.

questions.q: Kesari was founded in which western Indian city associated with Tilak?
questions.explanation: Kesari was established in Pune (Poona), the centre of Tilak's nationalist activity.

questions.q: The British colonial government responded to the nationalist press mainly by doing what?
questions.explanation: The government repeatedly restricted and prosecuted the nationalist press to silence it.

questions.q: Which leader is most closely linked with the slogan 'Swaraj is my birthright' published in his press?
questions.explanation: Tilak, editor of Kesari, famously declared 'Swaraj is my birthright and I shall have it.'

questions.q: In which year did Tilak begin publishing the newspapers Kesari and Maratha?
questions.explanation: Tilak, with associates, started Kesari and Maratha in 1881.

questions.q: Tilak was tried for sedition over articles in Kesari following which 1897 event in Pune?
options: The assassination of plague officer Rand
questions.explanation: After the 1897 killing of plague commissioner W. C. Rand, Tilak was tried for sedition over his Kesari writings.

questions.q: Tilak's most famous 1908 sedition trial centred on articles he wrote in Kesari defending whom?
questions.explanation: Tilak's 1908 trial arose from Kesari articles sympathetic to Bengal revolutionaries, leading to his deportation to Mandalay.

questions.q: Following his 1908 conviction over Kesari articles, Tilak was imprisoned in which prison in Burma?
questions.explanation: Tilak was sentenced to six years and deported to Mandalay jail in Burma after the 1908 trial.

questions.q: Which English-language nationalist newspaper was associated with Aurobindo Ghosh and Bipin Chandra Pal during the Swadeshi era?
questions.explanation: The English daily Bande Mataram, linked to Aurobindo Ghosh and Bipin Chandra Pal, was a leading Swadeshi-era paper.

questions.q: The Vernacular Press Act of 1878 was aimed at controlling newspapers published in which languages?
questions.explanation: The Vernacular Press Act of 1878 targeted the Indian-language press, exempting English papers.

questions.q: Which viceroy enacted the Vernacular Press Act of 1878 to muzzle the Indian-language press?
questions.explanation: Lord Lytton enacted the Vernacular Press Act in 1878; it was later repealed under Lord Ripon.

questions.q: Gandhi began editing Young India after taking over from which earlier owners?
options: A group of Home Rule activists in Bombay
questions.explanation: Gandhi took over Young India around 1919 from Home Rule activists and made it a vehicle for his ideas.

questions.q: Which Gujarati-language weekly did Gandhi edit alongside his English papers?
questions.explanation: Gandhi edited the Gujarati weekly Navajivan in parallel with Young India and Harijan.

questions.q: Tilak used Kesari to popularize which public festival as a means of building national feeling?
questions.explanation: Tilak promoted public Ganesh (and Shivaji) festivals through Kesari to foster a collective national spirit.

questions.q: The newspaper Amrita Bazar Patrika, a strong nationalist voice, was based primarily in which city?
questions.explanation: Amrita Bazar Patrika was a prominent nationalist newspaper based in Calcutta.

questions.q: During Tilak's imprisonment in Mandalay, what major work did he write?
questions.explanation: Tilak wrote the Gita Rahasya, his commentary on the Bhagavad Gita, during his Mandalay imprisonment.

questions.q: Who was the editor prosecuted in the first sedition case under Section 124A, the 1891 Bangobasi newspaper trial?
questions.explanation: Jogendra Chandra Bose of the Bangobasi was prosecuted in the first sedition case under Section 124A in 1891; the trial ended in a hung jury and was dropped after an apology, before Tilak's later convictions.

questions.q: In Tilak's 1897 sedition trial over Kesari, the disputed article concerned a lecture on which historical figure?
questions.explanation: The 1897 prosecution centred on a Kesari report of a Shivaji-festival speech the British read as inciting violence.

questions.q: The Newspaper (Incitement to Offences) Act of 1908 was passed largely in response to the writings of which kind of press?
questions.explanation: The 1908 Act targeted the extremist and revolutionary press, including papers sympathetic to Bengal militants.

questions.q: Surendranath Banerjee, jailed in 1883 for contempt over press criticism, was associated with which newspaper?
questions.explanation: Surendranath Banerjee edited The Bengalee and was imprisoned in 1883 for contempt of court over an article.

questions.q: The Indian Press Act of 1910, which forced newspapers to deposit security, was passed during the tenure of which viceroy?
questions.explanation: The Indian Press Act of 1910 was enacted under Lord Minto, requiring printers to deposit forfeitable security.

questions.q: Which barrister, later a major political leader, defended Tilak in his 1916 sedition case over his speeches and writings?
questions.explanation: Muhammad Ali Jinnah, then a Bombay barrister, defended Tilak in the 1916 sedition case and helped secure his acquittal; he was not Tilak's counsel in the 1908 trial.

questions.q: The Kesari and Maratha papers were originally launched by Tilak together with which co-founder of the Deccan Education Society?
questions.explanation: Gopal Ganesh Agarkar was the first editor of Kesari and a co-founder with Tilak before they parted over ideology.

questions.q: After Tilak's death in 1920, who took editorial responsibility associated with the Kesari group of papers?
questions.explanation: N. C. Kelkar, a close associate of Tilak, was a leading figure in carrying on the Kesari-Maratha papers after Tilak's death.
